attack_chain.description: Infostealer malware infected an Otelier employee's machine and harvested their Atlassian login credentials.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: These credentials were stolen through information-stealing malware

attack_chain.description: Threat actors used the stolen employee credentials to log in to Otelier's Atlassian (Bitbucket/Jira) server.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: they initially hacked the company's Atlassian server using an employee's login

evidence_chunks.quote: they initially hacked the company's Atlassian server using an employee's login
they initially hacked the company's Atlassian server using an employee's login

attack_chain.description: Attackers scraped tickets and other data inside Atlassian which contained additional credentials to the company's AWS S3 buckets.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: used these credentials to scrape tickets and other data, which contained further credentials to the company's S3 buckets

evidence_chunks.quote: used these credentials to scrape tickets and other data, which contained further credentials to the company's S3 buckets
used these credentials to scrape tickets and other data, which contained further credentials to the company's S3 buckets

attack_chain.description: Attackers used the harvested AWS credentials to authenticate to Otelier's Amazon S3 buckets.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: Using this access, the hackers claimed to have downloaded 7.8TB of data

evidence_chunks.quote: Using this access, the hackers claimed to have downloaded 7.8TB of data
Using this access, the hackers claimed to have downloaded 7.8TB of data

attack_chain.description: Threat actors downloaded approximately 7.8TB of data, including reservations, nightly reports, shift audits, and accounting data, from Otelier-managed S3 buckets.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: downloaded 7.8TB of data from the company's Amazon cloud storage

evidence_chunks.quote: downloaded 7.8TB of data from the company's Amazon cloud storage
downloaded 7.8TB of data from the company's Amazon cloud storage

attack_chain.description: Attackers left ransom notes inside the S3 buckets attempting to extort Marriott for cryptocurrency payment in exchange for not leaking the data.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: attempted to extort Marriott... left ransom notes requesting payment in cryptocurrency not to leak the data

attack_chain.description: Otelier rotated the compromised credentials in September 2024, terminating the attackers' access.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: they lost access in September after credentials were rotated

evidence_chunks.quote: they lost access in September after credentials were rotated
they lost access in September after credentials were rotated

initial_access_vector: Infostealer malware harvested an employee's Atlassian credentials, which the attackers used to log into Otelier's Atlassian server.
persistence_mechanism: Continued reuse of valid stolen credentials (Atlassian login and AWS access keys) until credentials were rotated in September 2024.
privilege_escalation: Discovered AWS S3 credentials embedded in Atlassian tickets/data, enabling pivot from corporate SaaS access to cloud storage access.
